summary: Among the most tournament-ready in the group after reaching the AFCON quarter-finals in January. Under Vladimir Petković, a transition threat — especially in wide areas — built on the running power of a young, talented attack, though with build-up and set-piece-defending frailties to fix.
style: A 4-3-3 (also 4-1-4-1 / 4-2-3-1) with dangerous movement in behind and a good high press. The wide runners (Chaibi, Amoura, Mahrez) stretch the pitch then drive inside to cross or finish, and the side is at its best threading through balls in behind for off-ball runs beyond the defence.

counterReliance.note: Transition threat in wide areas off a high press; dangerous in behind

strengths: Pace and running power out wide (Chaibi, Amoura, Mahrez) that drive inside to finish
Dangerous through balls in behind for off-ball runners
Good high press and a young, talented core (Aït-Nouri, Maza, Chaibi all 25 or under)
weaknesses: Weak in build-up — can be pressed into errors

keyPlayers.name: Mohamed Amoura
keyPlayers.note: 10 goals in qualifying — more than any other African player; versatile and blistering quick, with an unpredictable, varied shotmap

keyPlayers.name: Luca Zidane
keyPlayers.note: Son of Zinedine; switched eligibility to Algeria in September 2025 — set to play a World Cup almost 20 years after his father's last

tactics: Press high, move fluidly into wide areas, and break in behind with the front-line's pace — threading through balls for runners beyond the line. Fix the build-up and set-piece-defending frailties and they could make a real stab at the knockouts.
sources: The Athletic — 2026 World Cup tactical guide, Group J (5 Jun 2026)
John Walker (@johnwalker_1986) — pre-tournament tactical scouting, May–Jun 2026
